name: Bobby Moore
summary: England's only World Cup-winning captain. Moore's composure and reading of the game made him one of the finest defenders of his generation, and the image of him lifting the Jules Rimet Trophy in 1966 is the defining picture of English football.

name: Sir Geoff Hurst
summary: The only player to score a hat-trick in a World Cup final, a feat still unmatched sixty years on. Hurst's third goal against West Germany in 1966 remains the subject of debate over whether the ball crossed the line.

name: Sir Bobby Charlton
summary: A survivor of the Munich air disaster and one of England's greatest ever players, Charlton's 49 goals stood as the national record for nearly four decades, from 1970 until Wayne Rooney overtook it in 2015.

name: Gary Lineker
summary: The Golden Boot winner at the 1986 World Cup and England's spearhead through the late 1980s, Lineker scored the equaliser in the 1990 semi-final before penalties ended the run against West Germany.

name: Peter Shilton
summary: England's most-capped outfield goalkeeper for over three decades, Shilton's international career spanned five World Cups and ended with the 1990 semi-final defeat in Turin.

name: Paul Gascoigne
summary: Perhaps English football's most talented and most tragic figure. Gascoigne's tears after a booking ruled him out of a possible 1990 final, and his stunning volleyed goal against Scotland at Euro 96, made him a symbol of the era's brilliance and heartbreak in equal measure.

name: Gareth Southgate
summary: As a player, Southgate's missed penalty ended England's Euro 1996 dream. As manager two decades later, he rebuilt the team's culture from the ground up and delivered more major-tournament football than anyone since Alf Ramsey — a World Cup semi-final and two European Championship finals — without quite landing the trophy himself.

name: David Beckham
summary: England captain for six years, Beckham's whipped free-kick against Greece in 2001 sent England to the 2002 World Cup and became one of the most replayed moments in the team's history.

name: Wayne Rooney
summary: England's youngest ever goalscorer at the time of his debut, Rooney held the all-time scoring record from 2015 until Harry Kane passed him in 2023, a landmark Rooney himself presented Kane with in a gesture widely praised for its grace.

name: Harry Kane
summary: England's all-time record goalscorer and captain since 2018. Kane won the Golden Boot at the 2018 World Cup, has now led England to three major tournament finals or semi-finals in succession, and became England's all-time World Cup scorer during the 2026 tournament.

name: Jude Bellingham
summary: One of the most gifted midfielders of his generation, Bellingham has been the outstanding player of England's 2026 World Cup run, scoring the winner or equaliser in every knockout match through the quarter-finals, prompting comparisons to England's greatest ever players.
